title: teucrium scorodonia subsp. euganeum
intro: Teucrium scorodonia subsp. euganeum, also known as the alpine woodland germander, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is said to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Teucrium scorodonia subsp. euganeum for medicinal purposes.
